---
title: "power"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
# =============================================================================
# Simulation-Based Power Analysis: Personalized Pre-Session Preparation
# N-of-1 Crossover Trial | Maya Care and Grow, Agartala
# PI: Martin Braquet | Collaborator: Riki Dewan
# =============================================================================
#
# STRUCTURE
#   0.  Packages & parallel backend
#   1.  Blocked randomisation (block size = 4, no consecutive repeats)
#   2.  Data generation  [FIXED: prep + baseline assigned ONCE per session]
#   3.  Model fitting    [2 chains, 1000 iter — reliable individual-level CrI]
#   4.  Stopping criteria (per child)
#   5.  Fixed-N power sweep   → power curve
#   6.  Adaptive simulation   → expected sessions per child
#   7.  Reporting & plots
#   8.  Run
#
# COMPUTATIONAL NOTE
#   The adaptive arm refits the full hierarchical model at every interim for
#   every simulation.  Use n_sims_adaptive = 5–10 during development; bump to
#   50 for a final estimate.  The `update()` trick avoids Stan recompilation
#   after the first call — this is the single biggest speed-up available.
# =============================================================================

# NOTE: Selecting the best arm by posterior mean then checking its CrI
# introduces selection bias (the winner is inflated by being the maximum).
# This is partially mitigated by hierarchical shrinkage and the consecutive-
# interim requirement. See pre-registration for full justification.


# ── 0. Packages ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

library(tidyverse)
library(brms)
library(MASS)
library(furrr)
library(cmdstanr)
library(scales)

plan(multisession, workers = max(1L, parallel::detectCores() - 2L))

set.seed(20240101)


# ── 1. Blocked Randomisation ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
#
# Produces a condition sequence of length n_sessions with two constraints:
#   • Every block of 4 contains each condition exactly once (balanced).
#   • No condition is ever repeated consecutively across a block boundary.

PREP_LEVELS <- c("No Prep", "Calming", "Stimulating", "Child Choice")

generate_blocked_sequence <- function(n_sessions,
                                      conditions = PREP_LEVELS) {
  n_cond        <- length(conditions)
  blocks_needed <- ceiling(n_sessions / n_cond)
  sequence      <- character(0)

  for (b in seq_len(blocks_needed)) {
    # Shuffle until the first element does not repeat the last assigned condition
    repeat {
      block <- sample(conditions)
      if (length(sequence) == 0 || block[1] != tail(sequence, 1)) break
    }
    sequence <- c(sequence, block)
  }

  sequence[seq_len(n_sessions)]
}


# ── 2. Data Generation ───────────────────────────────────────────
#
# One row per (child × session × time_point).
# time_point is the only variable expanded within a session.

simulate_study_data <- function(n_children  = 25,
                                n_sessions  = 20,
                                effect_size = 0.5) {

  thresholds  <- c(-1.5, -0.5, 0.5, 1.5)

  child_rfx <- map_dfr(seq_len(n_children), function(id) {
    re <- MASS::mvrnorm(
      n     = 1,
      mu    = c(0, effect_size, effect_size, effect_size),
      Sigma = diag(c(0.2, 1.0, 1.0, 1.0))
    )
    tibble(
      child_id       = factor(id),
      re_intercept   = re[1],
      re_calming     = re[2],
      re_stimulating = re[3],
      re_childchoice = re[4]
    )
  })

  session_level <- map_dfr(seq_len(n_children), function(id) {
    tibble(
      child_id       = factor(id),
      session        = seq_len(n_sessions),
      prep           = factor(
        generate_blocked_sequence(n_sessions),
        levels = PREP_LEVELS
      ),
      baseline_state = factor(
        sample(c("Tired", "Neutral", "Hyper"), n_sessions, replace = TRUE),
        levels = c("Tired", "Neutral", "Hyper")
      )
    )
  })

  session_level %>%
    expand_grid(time_point = c("5min", "15min", "30min")) %>%
    left_join(child_rfx, by = "child_id") %>%
    mutate(
      # No fixed-effect winner — all active conditions treated equally
      # Small shared benefit of any prep over nothing (optional but realistic)
      fe_prep = if_else(prep == "No Prep", 0, 0.1),

      fe_time = if_else(time_point == "15min", 0.1, 0),

      fe_baseline = case_when(
        baseline_state == "Tired" ~ -0.3,
        baseline_state == "Hyper" ~  0.2,
        TRUE                      ~  0
      ),

      # Individual winner determined entirely by random slopes
      re_prep = case_when(
        prep == "Calming"      ~ re_calming,
        prep == "Stimulating"  ~ re_stimulating,
        prep == "Child Choice" ~ re_childchoice,
        TRUE                   ~ 0
      ),

      latent_score = re_intercept + fe_prep + fe_time +
                     fe_baseline + re_prep + rnorm(n(), 0, 0.7),

      engagement = as.integer(
        cut(latent_score,
            breaks = c(-Inf, thresholds, Inf),
            labels = FALSE)
      )
    ) %>%
    dplyr::select(child_id, session, prep, time_point, baseline_state, engagement)
}


# ── 3. Model Fitting ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
#
# Two chains and 500 post-warmup draws per chain give reliable individual-level
# 95% CrI (needed for per-child stopping decisions).
# Double-bar || keeps random intercept and slopes independent (no correlation
# term), matching the data-generation structure.
#
# PRIORS (fully specified — no implicit defaults)
#   β  ~ Normal(0, 1.5)   conservative for logit scale fixed effects
#   ζₖ ~ Normal(0, 3)     wide enough to span ±4 logit units (thresholds)
#   σᵤ ~ Exponential(1)   weakly regularising for random-effect SDs
#
# NOTE: LKJ prior is omitted because || removes the correlation structure.

SHARED_PRIOR <- c(
  prior(normal(0, 1.5), class = b),
  prior(normal(0, 3),   class = Intercept),
  prior(exponential(1), class = sd)
)

# Fits from scratch (used for first call per simulation)
fit_model_fresh <- function(data, prior = SHARED_PRIOR) {
  brm(
    engagement ~ prep + time_point + baseline_state +
      (1 + prep || child_id),
    data    = data,
    family  = cumulative("logit"),
    prior   = prior,
    chains  = 2,
    iter    = 1000,
    warmup  = 500,
    backend = "cmdstanr",
    threads = threading(2),
    refresh = 0,
    silent  = 2
  )
}

# Re-fits using a compiled template (avoids Stan recompilation — ~10× faster)
fit_model_update <- function(template_fit, new_data) {
  update(
    template_fit,
    newdata   = new_data,
    recompile = FALSE,
    chains    = 2,
    iter      = 1000,
    warmup    = 500,
    refresh   = 0,
    silent    = 2
  )
}


# ── 4. Per-Child Stopping Criteria ───────────────────────────────────────────

ROPE_LOWER <- -0.1
ROPE_UPPER <-  0.1


PREP_PARAMS <- c(
  Calming      = "prepCalming",
  Stimulating  = "prepStimulating",
  ChildChoice  = "prepChildChoice"
)

check_stopping_per_child <- function(fit,
                                     prob_best_threshold = 0.90,
                                     aipe_width          = 0.5) {

  draws     <- as_draws_df(fit)
  child_ids <- levels(fit$data$child_id)

  # Fixed-effect draws (population-level)
  b_cal  <- draws[["b_prepCalming"]]
  b_sti  <- draws[["b_prepStimulating"]]
  b_cc   <- draws[["b_prepChildChoice"]]

  map_dfr(child_ids, function(cid) {

    # Individual draws = fixed + random deviation
    # Column naming convention in brms: r_child_id[{id},prep{Level}]
    cal_draws <- b_cal + draws[[paste0("r_child_id[", cid, ",prepCalming]")]]
    sti_draws <- b_sti + draws[[paste0("r_child_id[", cid, ",prepStimulating]")]]
    cc_draws  <- b_cc  + draws[[paste0("r_child_id[", cid, ",prepChildChoice]")]]
    # No Prep = reference = 0 for every draw

    # Criterion 2: P(each arm is the best, including No Prep)
    n_draws      <- length(cal_draws)
    best_matrix  <- cbind(NoPrep = 0, cal_draws, sti_draws, cc_draws)
    winner_draw  <- max.col(best_matrix)   # 1=NoPrep, 2=Cal, 3=Sti, 4=CC

    p_best <- c(
      NoPrep      = mean(winner_draw == 1),
      Calming     = mean(winner_draw == 2),
      Stimulating = mean(winner_draw == 3),
      ChildChoice = mean(winner_draw == 4)
    )

    best_name <- names(which.max(p_best))

    # Superiority: modal winner has P(best) above threshold
    superiority <- max(p_best) >= prob_best_threshold

    # AIPE: CrI width for the leading arm is narrow enough
    if (best_name != "NoPrep") {
      best_draws <- list(
        Calming = cal_draws, Stimulating = sti_draws, ChildChoice = cc_draws
      )[[best_name]]
      cri_width <- as.numeric(quantile(best_draws, 0.975) -
                               quantile(best_draws, 0.025))
      aipe_met  <- cri_width < aipe_width
    } else {
      aipe_met <- FALSE
    }

    # ROPE: No Prep is the most probable best arm with high confidence
    in_rope <- p_best["NoPrep"] >= 0.70

    tibble(
      child_id       = cid,
      best_condition = best_name,
      p_best         = max(p_best),
      superiority    = superiority,
      aipe_met       = aipe_met,
      in_rope        = in_rope,
      stop_reason    = case_when(
        superiority          ~ "superiority",
        aipe_met             ~ "aipe",
        in_rope              ~ "rope",
        TRUE                 ~ "continue"
      )
    )
  })
}


# ── 5. Fixed-N Power Sweep ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
#
# For a grid of fixed session counts, runs n_sims simulations and estimates:
#
#   mean_ind_rate : Average proportion of children with detected effect.
#                   This is the "mean individual detection rate."
#
#   power         : Probability that ≥ thresh_pct of children are detected.
#                   The study-level power metric. Default threshold = 0.80.
#
# Both metrics are needed:
#   • mean_ind_rate answers "how personalised is the output on average?"
#   • power answers "will this study deliver clinically actionable results?"

fixed_n_one_sim <- function(sim_idx, n_sessions, effect_size,
                            template_fit, thresh_pct = 0.80) {
  sim_data <- simulate_study_data(n_sessions  = n_sessions,
                                  effect_size = effect_size)

  fit <- tryCatch(
    fit_model_update(template_fit, sim_data),
    error = function(e) NULL
  )
  if (is.null(fit)) return(NULL)

  checks <- tryCatch(
    check_stopping_per_child(fit),
    error = function(e) NULL
  )
  if (is.null(checks)) return(NULL)

  ind_rate <- mean(checks$superiority)
  print(ind_rate)
  tibble(
    sim        = sim_idx,
    ind_rate   = ind_rate,
    above_thresh = ind_rate >= thresh_pct
  )
}

run_fixed_n_sweep <- function(session_range = c(20, 30, 40),
                              n_sims        = 20,
                              effect_size   = 0.5,
                              thresh_pct    = 0.80,
                              template_fit) {

  map_dfr(session_range, function(n_s) {
    cat(sprintf("\n=== Fixed-N: %d sessions ===\n", n_s))

    sims <- future_map_dfr(
      seq_len(n_sims),
      fixed_n_one_sim,
      n_sessions   = n_s,
      effect_size  = effect_size,
      template_fit = template_fit,
      thresh_pct   = thresh_pct,
      .options     = furrr_options(seed = TRUE)
    )

    tibble(
      n_sessions    = n_s,
      mean_ind_rate = mean(sims$ind_rate,     na.rm = TRUE),
      power         = mean(sims$above_thresh, na.rm = TRUE),
      n_valid       = sum(!is.na(sims$ind_rate))
    )
  })
}


# ── 6. Adaptive Simulation ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
#
# Procedure (one simulation run):
#
#   a. Pre-generate max_sessions of data per child.
#   b. At each interim checkpoint (every `check_every` sessions, starting at
#      min_sessions), fit the hierarchical model on ALL data accumulated so far.
#      Partial pooling across children is preserved at every interim.
#   c. For each active child, apply stopping criteria:
#        • ROPE triggers immediately.
#        • Superiority must be confirmed at `consec_required` consecutive
#          interims before stopping (prevents premature stopping on noise).
#   d. All children still active when max_sessions is reached are labelled
#      "max_reached."
#   e. After all children have stopped, record sessions used and stopping reason.
#
# Returns a list with:
#   child_status   : per-child tibble (stop_session, stop_reason, detected)
#   mean_sessions  : mean sessions used per child in this simulation
#   p90_sessions   : 90th-percentile sessions (planning buffer)
#   detection_rate : proportion of children where best prep was detected

simulate_adaptive_one <- function(template_fit,
                                  n_children      = 25,
                                  min_sessions    = 12,
                                  max_sessions    = 60,
                                  check_every     = 4,
                                  effect_size     = 0.5,
                                  consec_required = 3) {

  # — Generate full data upfront —
  full_data <- simulate_study_data(n_children  = n_children,
                                   n_sessions  = max_sessions,
                                   effect_size = effect_size)

  # — Initialise child tracker —
  child_status <- tibble(
    child_id     = factor(seq_len(n_children)),
    stopped      = FALSE,
    stop_session = NA_integer_,
    stop_reason  = NA_character_,
    consec_sup   = 0L   # consecutive interims with superiority confirmed
  )

  # — Interim checkpoints —
  checkpoints <- seq(min_sessions, max_sessions, by = check_every)
  if (tail(checkpoints, 1) < max_sessions) checkpoints <- c(checkpoints, max_sessions)

  last_fit <- NULL   # carry forward for final detection check

  for (s in checkpoints) {
    active_ids <- child_status %>% filter(!stopped) %>% pull(child_id)
    if (length(active_ids) == 0) break

    # All children's data up to session s (pooling is always over full cohort)
    current_data <- full_data %>% filter(session <= s)

    fit <- tryCatch(
      fit_model_update(template_fit, current_data),
      error = function(e) NULL
    )
    if (is.null(fit)) next
    last_fit <- fit

    child_checks <- tryCatch(
      check_stopping_per_child(fit),
      error = function(e) NULL
    )
    if (is.null(child_checks)) next

    # — Update status for each active child —
    for (cid in as.character(active_ids)) {
      row <- child_checks %>% filter(child_id == cid)
      if (nrow(row) == 0) next

      idx <- which(child_status$child_id == cid)

      # Update consecutive superiority counter (reset if not confirmed)
      if (isTRUE(row$superiority)) {
        child_status$consec_sup[idx] <- child_status$consec_sup[idx] + 1L
      } else {
        child_status$consec_sup[idx] <- 0L
      }

      sustained_superiority <- child_status$consec_sup[idx] >= consec_required
      at_maximum            <- s == max_sessions

      stop_now <- sustained_superiority | isTRUE(row$in_rope) | at_maximum

      if (stop_now) {
        child_status$stopped[idx]      <- TRUE
        child_status$stop_session[idx] <- s
        child_status$stop_reason[idx]  <- case_when(
          sustained_superiority   ~ "superiority",
          isTRUE(row$in_rope)    ~ "rope",
          TRUE                    ~ "max_reached"
        )
      }
    }
  }

  # Use the last fitted model (at the session each child stopped) as proxy.
  # A separate final fit on per-child truncated data would be more precise
  # but prohibitively expensive for the sweep.
  detected <- if (!is.null(last_fit)) {
    tryCatch({
      ch <- check_stopping_per_child(last_fit)
      setNames(ch$superiority, ch$child_id)
    }, error = function(e) rep(NA, n_children))
  } else {
    rep(NA, n_children)
  }

  child_status <- child_status %>%
    mutate(detected = detected[as.character(child_id)])

  list(
    child_status   = child_status,
    mean_sessions  = mean(child_status$stop_session, na.rm = TRUE),
    p90_sessions   = as.numeric(quantile(child_status$stop_session, .90, na.rm = TRUE)),
    detection_rate = mean(child_status$detected, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
}

run_adaptive_sweep <- function(n_sims       = 10,
                               effect_size  = 0.5,
                               template_fit = NULL,   # pass compiled fit to avoid recompile
                               n_children   = 25,
                               min_sessions = 12,
                               max_sessions = 60,
                               check_every  = 4,
                               consec_required = 3) {

  if (is.null(template_fit)) {
    cat("Compiling Stan model for adaptive sweep...\n")
    init_data    <- simulate_study_data(n_sessions = min_sessions,
                                        effect_size = effect_size)
    template_fit <- fit_model_fresh(init_data)
  }

  cat(sprintf("\n=== Adaptive sweep: %d simulations ===\n", n_sims))
  cat("  (Each sim refits the model at every interim — allow ~10 min per sim)\n")

  results <- future_map(seq_len(n_sims), function(i) {
    cat(sprintf("  sim %d/%d\r", i, n_sims))
    tryCatch(
      simulate_adaptive_one(
        template_fit    = template_fit,
        n_children      = n_children,
        min_sessions    = min_sessions,
        max_sessions    = max_sessions,
        check_every     = check_every,
        effect_size     = effect_size,
        consec_required = consec_required
      ),
      error = function(e) NULL
    )
  }, .options = furrr_options(seed = TRUE))

  results <- Filter(Negate(is.null), results)
  results
}

# Summarise the list returned by run_adaptive_sweep()
summarise_adaptive <- function(results) {
  tibble(
    n_valid        = length(results),
    mean_sessions  = mean(map_dbl(results, "mean_sessions"),  na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_sessions    = sd(map_dbl(results, "mean_sessions"),    na.rm = TRUE),
    p50_sessions   = median(map_dbl(results, "mean_sessions"), na.rm = TRUE),
    p90_sessions   = mean(map_dbl(results, "p90_sessions"),   na.rm = TRUE),
    detection_rate = mean(map_dbl(results, "detection_rate"), na.rm = TRUE),

    # Stopping reason breakdown (averaged across sims)
    pct_superiority = mean(map_dbl(results, ~ {
      mean(.x$child_status$stop_reason == "superiority", na.rm = TRUE)
    }), na.rm = TRUE),
    pct_rope = mean(map_dbl(results, ~ {
      mean(.x$child_status$stop_reason == "rope", na.rm = TRUE)
    }), na.rm = TRUE),
    pct_max = mean(map_dbl(results, ~ {
      mean(.x$child_status$stop_reason == "max_reached", na.rm = TRUE)
    }), na.rm = TRUE)
  )
}


# ── 7. Reporting & Plots ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────

# 7a. Fixed-N power curve
plot_fixed_n <- function(results, target_rate = 0.80) {
  ggplot(results, aes(x = n_sessions)) +
    geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = 0, ymax = mean_ind_rate),
                fill = "#2c7bb6", alpha = 0.12) +
    geom_line(aes(y = mean_ind_rate, colour = "Mean individual\ndetection rate"),
              linewidth = 1.1) +
    geom_point(aes(y = mean_ind_rate, colour = "Mean individual\ndetection rate"),
               size = 3.5) +
    geom_line(aes(y = power, colour = "P(≥80% children\ndetected)"),
              linewidth = 1.1, linetype = "dashed") +
    geom_point(aes(y = power, colour = "P(≥80% children\ndetected)"),
               size = 3.5) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = target_rate, linetype = "dotted",
               colour = "grey40", linewidth = 0.7) +
    annotate("text",
             x = min(results$n_sessions) + 0.2,
             y = target_rate + 0.025,
             label = paste0(target_rate * 100, "% threshold"),
             colour = "grey30", hjust = 0, size = 3.5) +
    scale_colour_manual(values = c(
      "Mean individual\ndetection rate" = "#2c7bb6",
      "P(≥80% children\ndetected)"      = "#d7191c"
    ), name = NULL) +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1), limits = c(0, 1)) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = results$n_sessions) +
    labs(
      title    = "Fixed-N analysis: sessions needed to detect individual prep effects",
      subtitle = sprintf(
        "Effect size = 0.5 (logit) | %d simulations per point | 25 children",
        results$n_valid[1]),
      x        = "Sessions per child (fixed N)",
      y        = "Rate / Probability",
      caption  = paste0(
        "Blue = mean individual detection rate across sims.\n",
        "Red dashed = study-level power (P that ≥80% of children reach 95% CrI > 0)."
      )
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
    theme(legend.position = "top",
          plot.caption = element_text(colour = "grey50", size = 9))
}

# 7b. Distribution of sessions used per child under adaptive stopping
plot_session_distribution <- function(adaptive_results) {
  all_children <- map_dfr(seq_along(adaptive_results), function(i) {
    adaptive_results[[i]]$child_status %>%
      mutate(sim = i)
  })

  ggplot(all_children, aes(x = stop_session, fill = stop_reason)) +
    geom_histogram(binwidth = 2, colour = "white", alpha = 0.9,
                   position = "stack") +
    scale_fill_manual(
      values = c(
        superiority = "#1a9641",
        rope        = "#fdae61",
        max_reached = "#d7191c"
      ),
      labels = c(
        superiority = "Superiority confirmed",
        rope        = "No practical effect (ROPE)",
        max_reached = "Max sessions reached"
      ),
      name = "Stopping reason"
    ) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(12, 60, by = 4)) +
    labs(
      title    = "Distribution of sessions used per child — adaptive stopping",
      subtitle = paste0(
        "Green = stopped early with positive result | ",
        "Orange = stopped early with null result | Red = reached protocol maximum"),
      x        = "Session number when child stopped",
      y        = "Count  (children × simulations)"
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
    theme(legend.position = "bottom")
}

# 7c. Summary table printed to console
print_adaptive_summary <- function(summary_tbl) {
  cat("\n")
  cat("══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
  cat("  Adaptive Stopping Summary\n")
  cat("══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
  cat(sprintf("  Valid simulations:          %d\n",   summary_tbl$n_valid))
  cat(sprintf("  Mean sessions per child:    %.1f\n", summary_tbl$mean_sessions))
  cat(sprintf("  Median sessions per child:  %.1f\n", summary_tbl$p50_sessions))
  cat(sprintf("  90th-pct sessions:          %.1f\n", summary_tbl$p90_sessions))
  cat(sprintf("  Mean detection rate:        %.0f%%\n", summary_tbl$detection_rate * 100))
  cat("  ── Stopping reason breakdown ──\n")
  cat(sprintf("    Superiority:   %.0f%% of children\n", summary_tbl$pct_superiority * 100))
  cat(sprintf("    ROPE (no fx):  %.0f%% of children\n", summary_tbl$pct_rope       * 100))
  cat(sprintf("    Max reached:   %.0f%% of children\n", summary_tbl$pct_max        * 100))
  cat("══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")
}
```

```{r}
# ── 8. Run ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
#
# RECOMMENDED WORKFLOW
#
#   Phase 1 (development, <1 hr):
#     n_sims        = 10,  session_range = c(20, 30, 40)
#     n_sims_adapt  = 5
#
#   Phase 2 (final estimate, overnight):
#     n_sims        = 100, session_range = c(20, 25, 30, 35, 40)
#     n_sims_adapt  = 50

N_SIMS_FIXED  <- 10
N_SIMS_ADAPT  <- 5
EFFECT_SIZE   <- 0.5
SESSION_RANGE <- c(36, 40)

cat("Compiling Stan model...\n")
template_fit <- fit_model_fresh(
  simulate_study_data(n_sessions = min(SESSION_RANGE), effect_size = EFFECT_SIZE)
)

# — 8a. Fixed-N sweep —
fixed_results <- run_fixed_n_sweep(
  session_range = SESSION_RANGE,
  n_sims        = N_SIMS_FIXED,
  effect_size   = EFFECT_SIZE,
  thresh_pct    = 0.80,
  template_fit  = template_fit
)

cat("\n── Fixed-N Results ──────────────────────────────────\n")
print(fixed_results)

p_fixed <- plot_fixed_n(fixed_results, target_rate = 0.80)
print(p_fixed)
ggsave("power_curve_fixed_n.png", p_fixed, width = 9, height = 5.5, dpi = 150)
```

```{r}
# — 8b. Adaptive sweep —
adaptive_results <- run_adaptive_sweep(
  n_sims          = N_SIMS_ADAPT,
  effect_size     = EFFECT_SIZE,
  template_fit    = template_fit,
  n_children      = 25,
  min_sessions    = 12,
  max_sessions    = 36,
  check_every     = 4,
  consec_required = 3
)

adaptive_summary <- summarise_adaptive(adaptive_results)
print_adaptive_summary(adaptive_summary)

p_sessions <- plot_session_distribution(adaptive_results)
print(p_sessions)
ggsave("sessions_distribution_adaptive.png", p_sessions, width = 9, height = 5.5, dpi = 150)

# — 8c. Combined figure (for paper / portfolio) —
if (requireNamespace("gridExtra", quietly = TRUE)) {
  combined_plot <- gridExtra::arrangeGrob(
    p_fixed, p_sessions,
    ncol = 1,
    top  = grid::textGrob(
      "N-of-1 power analysis: fixed vs adaptive design\nMaya Care and Grow | Bayesian hierarchical cumulative logit model",
      gp = grid::gpar(fontsize = 13, fontface = "bold")
    )
  )
  ggsave("combined_power_analysis.png", combined_plot,
         width = 10, height = 11, dpi = 150)
  cat("Combined figure saved to combined_power_analysis.png\n")
} else {
  cat("Install gridExtra for a combined figure: install.packages('gridExtra')\n")
  cat("Individual figures already saved as separate PNGs.\n")
}

cat("\nAll done. Output PNGs saved to working directory.\n")
```

```{r}
data <- simulate_study_data(n_children = 4, n_sessions  = 36, effect_size = 0.5)
data$baseline_state <- 'hyper'
data
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)

result <- data %>%
  group_by(child_id, prep) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_engagement = mean(engagement, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_engagement   = sd(engagement, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

result
```

```{r}

```

```{r}
ggplot(data, aes(x = prep, y = engagement, fill = prep)) +
  # Use boxplots to show distribution
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.6, outlier.shape = NA) +
  # Add jittered points to show every single session's data
  geom_jitter(width = 0.2, height = 0.1, alpha = 0.5, size = 1.5) +
  # Facet by Child (Rows) and Baseline State (Columns)
  facet_grid(baseline_state ~ child_id) +
  # Styling
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = 1:5, limits = c(0.5, 5.5)) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Personalized Prep Impact on Session Engagement",
    subtitle = "Faceted by Child and Arrival Baseline State",
    x = "Preparation Type",
    y = "BRES-5 Engagement Score",
    fill = "Condition"
  ) +
  theme(
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "gray95"),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    legend.position = "none"
  )
```

```{r}
template_fit
```

```{r}
ci_width <- 0.9
lo <- (1 - ci_width) / 2
hi <- 1 - lo
probs_needed <- c(lo, hi)

child_ests <- coef(fit, probs = probs_needed)$child_id
lo_tag     <- paste0("Q", lo * 100)
hi_tag     <- paste0("Q", hi * 100)
child_ids  <- dimnames(child_ests)[[1]]

cid <- 0

map_dbl(PREP_PARAMS, ~ child_ests[cid, "Estimate", .x])
```
